title: Comparison of CNN vs. MLP on Fixed Splits for Protein Expression Prediction
scientific_hypothesis: The Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) will achieve a higher R² score compared to the Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) on fixed per-series held-out sets.
expected_failure_modes: Overfitting due to high model complexity
Insufficient training data for CNN to learn effectively
MLP outperforming CNN if the input features are not spatially structured

title: Comparison of RF vs. MLP on Fixed Splits for Protein Expression Prediction
scientific_hypothesis: The Random Forest (RF) will achieve a higher R² score compared to the Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) on fixed per-series held-out sets.
expected_failure_modes: RF's interpretability issues leading to suboptimal feature selection
MLP outperforming RF if the input features are highly non-linear
Overfitting in RF due to too many trees or deep trees

title: Comparison of CNN vs. RF on Fixed Splits for Protein Expression Prediction
scientific_hypothesis: The Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) will achieve a higher R² score compared to the Random Forest (RF) on fixed per-series held-out sets.
expected_failure_modes: CNN's inability to handle non-spatial data effectively
RF outperforming CNN if the input features are highly categorical or sparse
Overfitting in CNN due to excessive filter sizes or layers
